data.title: LS Heavy Equipment to Acquire Related Firm's Unit for 100 Million Yuan; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Lanzhou LS Heavy Equipment , through its unit Qingdao Lanshi Heavy Machinery Equipment, will acquire Qingdao Lanshi Petroleum Equipment Engineering for almost 100 million yuan.

Qingdao Lanshi will be bought from related company Lanzhou Lanshi Petroleum Equipment Engineering.

After the completion of the transaction, Qingdao Lanshi will become a third-tier subsidiary of the Chinese heavy machinery manufacturer.

The company's shares jumped over 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Offshore Yuan Ends 2-Session Decline
data.description:
The offshore yuan rose to around 7.18 per dollar on Thursday, snapping a two-session losing streak, supported by a weakening US dollar.

The greenback depreciated after US President Trump announced plans to send formal letters to major trading partners in the coming weeks, expected to outline unilateral tariffs aimed at pressuring countries into trade deals.

Adding to the pressure on the greenback was softer-than-expected inflation data in May, fueling speculation that the Fed could begin cutting interest rates as early as September.

Elsewhere, the yuan gained additional strength from the conclusion of a two-day round of US-China trade talks in London.

The discussions resulted in a framework agreement focused on implementing the Geneva consensus and advancing commitments made during a recent presidential phone call.

In China, market attention is shifting to a set of key economic indicators due next week, including data on industrial production, retail sales, and the unemployment rate.

data.title: Pepco Group Completes Sale Of Poundland To Gordon Brothers
data.description:
Pepco Group NV :

  • SALE OF POUNDLAND TO GORDON BROTHERS COMPLETED

  • TRANSACTION ENABLES GROUP TO FOCUS ON HIGHER GROWTH AND HIGHER MARGIN PEPCO BUSINESS

  • THE SHARES IN POUNDLAND HAVE BEEN SOLD TO GORDON BROTHERS FOR NOMINAL CONSIDERATION, WITH PEPCO'S SECURED LOAN OF £30M AND CERTAIN UNSECURED LOANS REMAINING IN PLACE BETWEEN PEPCO GROUP AND POUNDLAND

data.title: CITIC Gets Green Light to Extend Sino Iron Operations
data.description:
CITIC said it received ministerial approval from the Western Australian government to proceed with interim expansion plans for its Sino Iron Project, according to a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing.

CITIC's project companies, Sino Iron and Korean Steel, submitted the 2023 Mine Continuation Proposals in May alongside tenement holder Mineralogy.

The proposals, which cover pit extension and tailings and waste rock storage within existing areas, were approved unconditionally on June 9, the filing said.

The approval allows CITIC to continue operating the project within areas already made available by Mineralogy.

Pepco Group N.V. (Pepco and, together with its subsidiaries, Pepco Group or the Group), a leading

data.title: Tesco Sales Rise as Market Share Increases Despite Tough Competition
data.description:
(06:01 GMT) *Tesco 1Q Sales GBP16.38B
(06:01 GMT) *Tesco 1Q LFL Sales Rose 4.6%
(06:01 GMT) *Tesco 1Q UK LFL Sales Rose 5.1%
(06:01 GMT) *Tesco 1Q Central Europe LFL Sales Rose 4.1%
(06:02 GMT) *Tesco Backs FY26 View
(06:02 GMT) *Tesco Sees FY26 Group Adj Oper Pft GBP2.7B-GBP3.0B
(06:03 GMT) *Tesco Sees FY26 Retail FCF Within Midterm Guidance GBP1.4B-GBP1.8B
(06:04 GMT) *Tesco 1Q Booker Sales Up 2%
(06:04 GMT) *Tesco 1Q Booker Sales GBP2.31B
(06:34 GMT) Tesco Sales Rise as Market Share Increases Despite Tough Competition

By Andrea Figueras

U.K. grocer Tesco maintained its full-year expectations after sales climbed in its first quarter, when it continued to gain market share despite fierce competition in the sector.

The supermarket on Thursday posted 16.38 billion pounds ($22.19 billion) in group sales excluding value-added tax and fuel for the 13 weeks ended May 24, rising 4.6% on a like-for-like basis.

Like-for-like sales in its home market rose 5.1%, and edged up 4.1% in central Europe.

The supermarket chain continues to expect adjusted operating profit--its preferred metric, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--to come in at 2.7 billion to 3.0 billion pounds in fiscal 2026 compared with the 3.13 billion pounds it reported for the year ended Feb. 22.

The group said it continued to see market-share gains in what it described as an intensely competitive market.

Coupled with fierce competition, the U.K. grocery sector faces increased costs, including employer payroll tax contributions in the U.K. In October, in her first budget as U.K. Treasury Chief, Rachel Reeves increased the national living wage and employers' national insurance contributions.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Savani Financials Raises INR50 Million Through Debenture Allotment
data.description:
Savani Financials has raised 50 million Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible debentures, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company allotted unlisted and unrated 500 non-convertible debentures of a face value of 100,000 rupees each to the allottee.

The debentures carry a coupon rate of 14% with payable interest on a monthly basis. These are scheduled to mature in 36 months.

The company's shares were up 2% in recent trade.

data.title: U.K. Economy Shrinks on Trump Tariff Hit — Update
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

The U.K. economy contracted more than expected in April as President Trump's evolving trade policy brought a halt to a surprising surge in activity.

Gross domestic product was 0.3% lower than a month earlier, the country's Office for National Statistics said Thursday, marking the deepest contraction in the British economy in 18 months. Economists had expected a shallower contraction of 0.1%, according to a poll compiled by The Wall Street Journal ahead of the release.

Production at the country's factories decreased in April, after having risen strongly at the start of the year as American importers rushed to stock up ahead of an anticipated tariff package at the start of the month. Exports to the U.S. dropped by the fastest rate on record following the introduction of those tariffs, the ONS said.

Prior to the April contraction, Europe's second-largest economy had enjoyed a brisk start to 2025 as it grew by 0.7% in the first quarter, outpacing the eurozone and the U.S., where output contracted over the same period.

U.K. industrial production fell 0.6% on the month after President Trump set out levies on the U.S.'s largest trading partners, including the U.K, at the start of April.

London and Washington later inked a deal that will allow British exports to avoid the higher range of Trump's trade duties. U.K. goods will face a baseline tariff of 10%, lower than the 20% threatened on European Union exports, with exemptions for some key goods such as steel.

Still, the frostier global trade environment has begun to take its toll on the British economy. As well as the downturn in factory output, the cloudy horizon is leading firms to delay investment decisions and trim workforces.

"You've got a lot of caution on the business side, particularly in investment," said Alpesh Paleja, deputy chief economist at the Confederation of British Industry.

"That's the area most affected by uncertainty, and the increase in global trade barriers," Paleja told The Wall Street Journal.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Adviser Banned 10 Years After $15M Hit Shady UK Platform
data.description:
Australia's financial regulator has banned a former financial adviser for 10 years after she funneled at least $14.8 million of client money into a cryptocurrency scam while telling investors their funds were going into safe, fixed-interest accounts.

ASIC Bans Financial Adviser for Crypto Scam That Cost Clients $14.8 Million

The Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) said Glenda Maree Rogan violated financial services laws through misleading conduct during her time as an adviser with the Fincare group of companies in Sutherland Shire and Wollongong.

Between March 2022 and June 2023, Rogan transferred client funds to bank accounts she controlled, then converted the money to cryptocurrency and sent it to wallets linked to something called the Financial Centre, which claimed to be a UK-based trading platform. ASIC has the Financial Centre on its investor alert list as an unlicensed entity that shouldn't be trusted.

Rogan told clients their money was going into high-yield fixed interest accounts rather than risky cryptocurrency investments. She also falsely claimed clients were dealing with her as a representative of Fincare, according to ASIC's findings.

The regulator determined Rogan likely had suspicions about the Financial Centre's legitimacy from at least October 2022 but continued directing client money there anyway.

Comprehensive Ban Imposed

The 10-year ban prevents Rogan from providing any financial services or controlling entities that offer such services. The prohibition took effect June 6 and has been recorded on ASIC's banned and disqualified register.

Rogan worked as both an accountant and financial adviser at Fincare Planning Pty Ltd, Fincare Private Wealth Pty Ltd and Fincare Accounting Pty Ltd. She served as a director of those companies at various times between May 2014 and February 2024.

She was an authorized representative of Australian Financial Services licensee Private Wealth Pty Ltd from November 2020 until February 2024, when her authorization ended. She hasn't been licensed to provide financial services since then.

“ASIC also found it had reason to believe that Ms Rogan is not a fit and proper person, is not competent to participate in the Australian financial services industry and is likely to contravene a financial services law,” the market watchdog commented.

Rogan can appeal the decision to the Administrative Review Tribunal. Affected investors can file complaints with the Australian Financial Complaints Authority.

Crypto Scams on the Rise

Cryptocurrency-related investment fraud continues to pose a growing threat to retail investors in Australia, often through seemingly legitimate financial advisory channels. In April, ASIC moved to shut down 95 firms linked to online investment and romance scams, commonly referred to as “pig butchering” schemes, following a court order.

These scams typically involve fraudsters who impersonate others on social media platforms, gaining victims' trust over weeks or months before introducing them to high-risk investments such as cryptocurrencies or contracts for differences (CFDs). According to The Economist, these operations are often coordinated by criminal groups based in countries like Myanmar.

Despite a decline in the number of scam reports, financial losses from fraud in Australia surged in early 2025. Between January and April, Australians submitted 72,230 scam reports—down 24% year-on-year—yet total reported losses rose by 29%, reaching nearly A$119 million. Investment scams were the primary driver, accounting for more than half of the total losses.

Phishing attacks and social media scams also remain prevalent, with fraudsters using digital platforms to target individuals across all age groups. Authorities warn that scam tactics continue to evolve, making detection and prevention increasingly difficult.
